---
title: "Tidy update: IOPAC-network-map-script-cciea"
format: html
---

## --- Setup & Directories ---
library(tidyverse)
library(ggmap)
library(cowplot)
library(here)
library(magick)

# Configuration
thisyear <- 2025
most_recent_igraph <- "2026-03-10"
png_pattern <- paste0("2025_circular_compare_", most_recent_igraph)

indir_ports <- 'data/networks/participation_vessel_ports/plots/comparable/2025 only/.'
esr_dir     <- "/Users/ana.guerra/Documents/CONFIDENTIAL/Outputs/CCIEA Networks/Figures/"
esr_output  <- TRUE 

## --- Efficient Image Loading ---

# Get all matching files at once
all_files <- list.files(here(indir_ports), pattern = png_pattern, full.names = TRUE)

# Define a mapping of short names to search patterns
port_patterns <- c(
  nwc = "North WA Coast", ps = "Puget Sound", wc = "(?<!North )WA Coast",
  ast = "Astoria", tl = "Tillamook", nwp = "Newport", cb = "Coos Bay", 
  brk = "Brookings", cr = "Columbia River", cc = "Crescent City", 
  eu = "Eureka", fb = "Fort Bragg", bb = "Bodega Bay", sf = "San Francisco", 
  mt = "Monterey", sd = "San Diego", sb = "Santa Barbara", la = "Los Angeles", 
  mb = "Morro Bay"
)

# Load all images into a named list using functional programming
# This replaces ~100 lines of repetitive img_xxx <- image_read() code
imgs <- map(port_patterns, function(pat) {
  match <- all_files[str_detect(all_files, regex(pat, perl = TRUE))]
  if(length(match) > 0) image_read(match[1]) else NULL
})

# Load coordinate reference
box <- read_csv(here('data', 'input', 'node_box_ref_esr2026.csv'))

## --- Consolidated Mapping Logic ---

base_map_iopac <- function(state, xlim = c(-130, -117), ylim = c(NA, NA)) {
  states <- map_data("state") %>% filter(region %in% state)
  ggplot(states, aes(long, lat, group = group)) +
    geom_polygon(fill = "gray90", alpha = 0.5, color = "gray85") +
    coord_map(xlim = xlim, ylim = ylim) +
    theme_void()
}

box_iopac <- function(basemap, region_code) {
  region_boxes <- box %>% filter(map_version == region_code)
  
  basemap +
    geom_point(data = region_boxes, aes(PRIMARY_LONGITUDE, PRIMARY_LATITUDE, fill = STATE), 
               colour = "black", pch = 21, size = 2) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = region_boxes, aes(PRIMARY_LONGITUDE, PRIMARY_LATITUDE, label = IOPAC),
                            size = 2.15, box.padding = 0) +
    geom_rect(data = region_boxes, aes(xmax = xmax, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, ymin = ymin),
              fill = NA, colour = "darkgray") +
    # Combined segments logic using a helper long-format if necessary, 
    # but keeping your logic simplified:
    geom_segment(data = region_boxes, aes(x = PRIMARY_LONGITUDE, y = PRIMARY_LATITUDE, 
                                          xend = xmax, yend = ymax), color = 'darkgray') +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c('gray', 'black', 'white')) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}

## --- Generate and Save Coastwide Figure ---

CW_map <- ggdraw(box_iopac(base_map_iopac(c("washington", "oregon", "california")), 'CW')) +
  draw_image(imgs$ps,  x = .06, y = .66, width = 0.31, height = 0.31) +
  draw_image(imgs$nwp, x = .08, y = .43, width = 0.31, height = 0.31) +
  draw_image(imgs$cc,  x = .08, y = .18, width = 0.31, height = 0.31) +
  draw_image(imgs$eu,  x = .27, y = .29, width = 0.31, height = 0.31)

# Helper for saving
save_map <- function(p, name, w = 6, h = 10) {
  local_path <- here(indir_ports, paste0(name, '_', thisyear, '.png'))
  ggsave(local_path, p, width = w, height = h, dpi = 600)
  if(esr_output) ggsave(paste0(esr_dir, name, '-', thisyear, '.png'), p, width = w, height = h, dpi = 600)
}

save_map(CW_map, "iopac-node_full-coast_rect_font")
